channel.title: QuickTune
channel.description: Most recent changes with links to updates.

item.description:
Apple Music 1.6, bundled with macOS Tahoe, changed the data set sent with inter-app communication. Expected information when track or player state changed was no longer sent, causing QuickTune to behave incorrectly. Although Apple never reverted to the old behavior, it took some investigation to figure out how to get QuickTune working again, while continuing to keep compatibility with previous versions of macOS. Thank you for your patience during this time.

New in this update:

  • Support for macOS Tahoe and Apple Music 1.6.
  • App menu item icons (only in macOS 26.0 and newer).
  • QuickTune is 100% "hand-made." No use of AI went into designing, developing, or testing QuickTune.

Apple Music 1.6, bundled with macOS Tahoe, changed the data set sent with inter-app communication. Expected information when track or player state changed was no longer sent, causing QuickTune to behave incorrectly. Although Apple never reverted to the old behavior, it took some investigation to figure out how to get QuickTune working again, while continuing to keep compatibility with previous versions of macOS. Thank you for your patience during this time.

New in this update:

  • Support for macOS Tahoe and Apple Music 1.6.
  • App menu item icons (only in macOS 26.0 and newer).
  • QuickTune is 100% "hand-made." No use of AI went into designing, developing, or testing QuickTune.

• Updated to display proper names for Apple Música Uno, Apple Music Chill, and Apple Music Club.
  • Fixed an issue where the play/stop glyph wouldn't display properly based on media playback types.
